name: Walgreens Hub – Business Objects & Processes
description: Flashcards covering core business objects, workflows, and operational processes within Walgreens Hub Solutions and associated pharmacy operations.

cards.question: What is the primary business purpose of the Walgreens Hub?

a.text: To coordinate patient support activities such as benefits investigation, prior authorization, affordability programs, and adherence monitoring.
a.explanation: This reflects the Hub's core role as a centralized patient-support and workflow coordination platform.

b.text: To replace all specialty pharmacy locations with a fully virtual dispensing model.
b.explanation: The Hub does not replace dispensing pharmacies; it supports them.

c.text: To serve as an inventory management system for Walgreens retail stores.
c.explanation: Inventory management occurs in dispensing systems, not the Hub.

d.text: To automate prescriber credentialing and licensing workflows.
d.explanation: The Hub does not perform regulatory credentialing functions.

cards.explanation: Walgreens Hub Solutions centralizes high-touch patient support activities—insurance verification, PA processing, affordability, outreach, and clinical support—to accelerate time to therapy and ensure adherence.

cards.question: What is the purpose of the Receive → Rescue → Redirect model?

a.text: To identify hub-eligible prescriptions and enroll patients in support programs before returning the prescription to a dispensing pharmacy.
a.explanation: This is the Hub’s patient-support flow, ensuring support utilization.

b.text: To instruct retail pharmacists on resolving medication stock shortages.
b.explanation: Stock shortage workflows are outside the Hub model.

c.text: To route calls between Twilio IVR segments for proper agent assignment.
c.explanation: Twilio routing is separate from the Hub’s business model.

d.text: To escalate financial assistance cases to manufacturers for rebate processing.
d.explanation: The model is about patient support flow, not rebate escalation.

cards.explanation: The Receive–Rescue–Redirect model ensures that prescriptions entering Walgreens’ network are reviewed for Hub eligibility, supported through Hub processes, and ultimately returned to dispensing pharmacies once support tasks are completed.

cards.question: What business object represents the complete workflow state of a prescription inside Scriptly?

a.explanation: Statuses and Substatuses track workflow stage, escalation needs, clinical steps, PA stages, etc.

b.explanation: Program type indicates fill type (e.g., QuickStart, PAP), not workflow state.

c.explanation: Providers use their portal, but workflow state is tracked separately.

d.explanation: Engagement tasks do not represent full workflow state.

cards.explanation: Scriptly uses a detailed Status/Substatus framework to indicate where an Rx sits in intake, BI, IV, PA, Appeals, PAP, and outreach workflows—supporting reporting and turn-around-time tracking.

cards.question: What is the purpose of the 'Program' object in Scriptly?

a.text: To classify the fill-type pathway for the patient such as Traditional, QuickStart, Bridge, or PAP.
a.explanation: The Program object defines manufacturer-supported program types associated with an order.

b.text: To represent the prescriber’s specialty and clinical area.
b.explanation: Prescriber attributes are separate from Program.

c.text: To define pharmacy inventory segmentation tiers.
c.explanation: Inventory is not tied to Program objects.

d.text: To define call-routing rules for Twilio Flex.
d.explanation: Call routing is unrelated to clinical Program classification.

cards.explanation: Programs indicate fill-type variants (QuickStart, Bridge, PAP, etc.) used to manage affordability, eligibility, and manufacturer-required data tracking across a prescription’s lifecycle.

cards.question: Which business object identifies whether a prescription has been transferred out of the Hub to a dispensing pharmacy?

a.explanation: Referral flags record that the Hub has transferred the script to another pharmacy and continue monitoring.

b.explanation: Tasks do not identify transfer status.

c.explanation: This tracks provider metadata, not transfer events.

d.explanation: PAR levels are irrelevant to transfers.

cards.explanation: The Referral flag and its sub-statuses track transferred prescriptions and maintain compliance while supporting ongoing patient outreach and assistance after transfer.

cards.question: What is the role of the Prior Authorization (PA) workflow object?

a.text: To document insurer-required clinical or administrative steps needed before a specialty medication can be dispensed.
a.explanation: PA workflows coordinate insurer requirements, documentation, outreach, and approvals.

b.text: To determine medication shipping logistics for cold-chain distribution.
b.explanation: Shipping is unrelated to PA approvals.

c.text: To assign refill reminders and adherence calls on a monthly schedule.
c.explanation: Those are adherence workflows, not PA workflows.

d.explanation: Rebates are not managed inside PA workflows.

cards.explanation: PA workflows include: PA Initiated → Pending HCP → Pending Insurance → Approved/Denied, with substatus variants. These are critical for enabling coverage and therapy initiation.

cards.question: What is the purpose of the Max PA Status field?

a.text: To preserve the most current PA/Appeal/MTE stage even if the main prescription Status/Substatus temporarily moves to another workflow (e.g., QuickStart or Scheduling).
a.explanation: Max PA Status ensures reporting accuracy when the Rx workflow diverges.

b.text: To track the maximum number of PA attempts allowed by an insurer.
b.explanation: Insurer rules are not stored in Max PA Status.

c.text: To record the highest clinical severity level for the medication.
c.explanation: Clinical severity is unrelated to PA workflows.

d.text: To determine which agent has the highest PA completion rate.
d.explanation: This is performance analytics, not PA logic.

cards.explanation: Max PA Status is required because the Rx may move to other statuses (e.g., patient outreach), but reporting must still reflect ongoing PA/Appeal progress for manufacturers.

cards.question: Which business object controls agent permissions inside Scriptly?

a.explanation: Scriptly is transitioning to team-based permissioning, where teams define access scopes.

b.explanation: Location does not grant functional permissions.

c.explanation: This applies to prescribers, not agents.

d.explanation: Inventory groups are not permission sets.

cards.explanation: Teams define what UI sections and actions a user can access, including intake, PA workflows, compliance tasks, dispensing review, and program management.

cards.question: What is the purpose of the 'Dispensing SP' field in the PROMOTE referral workflow?

a.text: To record the pharmacy where the Hub instructs the prescription to be filled after Hub services are complete.
a.explanation: Dispensing SP identifies the final dispensing location.

b.text: To determine whether the medication should be placed on backorder.
b.explanation: Backorders are not controlled via this field.

c.text: To specify which Hub agent performed the triage.

d.text: To select the financial assistance program used for copay support.
d.explanation: Assistance programs do not use this field.

cards.explanation: PROMOTE's 'Dispensing SP' field stores the pharmacy where the prescription is filled after Hub intervention, enabling auditing and preventing accidental re-transfer.

cards.question: What does the Ingestion Queue represent in the Hub workflow?

a.text: The initial intake location where incoming prescriptions are timestamped and prepared for data entry and BI/IV tasks.
a.explanation: Ingestion begins the formal Scriptly workflow and is used for TAT metrics.

b.text: A queue for unassigned financial assistance cases.
b.explanation: FA cases are separate from ingestion.

c.text: A storage area for insurance plan definitions.
c.explanation: Plan configs do not pass through ingestion.

d.text: A Twilio-interrupted call queue used for callback routing.
d.explanation: Twilio queues are not involved in Rx ingestion.

cards.explanation: The Ingestion Queue timestamps incoming orders and supports turn-around-time calculations for Intake → BI → PA workflows.
